part_description: Divine names and attributes surfaced by the STEP related-words cluster for sha.lom. These terms sit at the Framework A/B boundary: they are not soul-words in the standard Framework B sense but represent the divine-nature side of a potential Framework A/B intersection. Extracted for future discovery and cross-framework synthesis. No Session B analysis to be conducted until the full dataset is assembled and the intersection question is formally opened.
phase2_synthesis_note: Key question for synthesis stage: if YHWH himself is peace (YHWH-Shalom), righteousness (YHWH-Tsidkenu), the jealous guardian (qan.na), the warrior who establishes peace (tsa.va) — what does this imply about the origin and nature of the corresponding soul characteristics in Framework B?
source_file_notes: Source file contains word analysis blocks for all 8 terms but no anchor verses were captured in STEP. Verses recorded as empty arrays with ANCHOR_VERSES_NOT_CAPTURED flag. Anchor verse extraction to be completed in a follow-up STEP session.
The STEP meaning block for all divine-name terms is a single composite entry covering all names/titles of the Deity. Term-specific sense extracted from the inline gloss (§ note) where present.
she.lam is XREF only — full extraction held in Part 1.

data_quality_flags.note: No anchor verses were captured in the source file for any Part 3 term. All verse arrays are empty. Follow-up STEP session required to curate 2–4 anchor verses per term.

terms.anchor_note: Root divine name — ontological ground for all compound YHWH-names in this part. ~10,284 OT occurrences; 22 NT occurrences. Anchor verse extraction deferred.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: The personal name of the God of Israel; the self-existent, eternal One; root of all compound YHWH divine names
senses: The Deity — the personal name of Israel's God
KJV: Jehovah; standard English translations: LORD (small caps)
Root of compound names: YHWH-Shalom, YHWH-Tsidkenu, YHWH-Nissi, YHWH-Jireh, YHWH-Sabaoth
Also rendered: LORD (H3069), LORD/Yah (H3050), Lord/Adonai (H0136), God/Elohim (H0430G)

terms.anchor_note: Name of the altar built by Gideon in Ophrah (Judg 6:24). Declares YHWH as the ontological source of peace. Single OT occurrence as a place-name/altar-name.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: YHWH is Peace — name of the sacred altar built by Gideon in Ophrah; declares God himself as peace
senses: Name of a sacred altar built by Gideon in Ophrah (Judg 6:24)
Theological declaration: YHWH is (the source of) peace
Compound: YHWH (H3068G) + shalom peace (H7965H)

terms.verses_note: ANCHOR_VERSES_NOT_CAPTURED — follow-up STEP extraction required. Primary anchor: Judg 6:24.

terms.anchor_note: Messianic title applied to both the coming king (Jer 23:6) and to Jerusalem (Jer 33:16). YHWH himself is the righteousness of his people.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: YHWH our Righteousness — messianic title; a sacred name applied to Jerusalem and the Messiah
senses: YHWH our righteousness — Jehovah is our righteousness
Applied to the coming Davidic king: Jer 23:6
Applied to Jerusalem: Jer 33:16
Compound: YHWH (H3068G) + tsidqenu our righteousness (H6664H)

terms.verses_note: ANCHOR_VERSES_NOT_CAPTURED — follow-up STEP extraction required. Primary anchors: Jer 23:6; Jer 33:16.

terms.anchor_note: Name of the altar built by Moses to commemorate the defeat of the Amalekites (Exod 17:15). YHWH as the warrior-banner who gives victory and establishes peace through conquest.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: YHWH is my Banner — name of the altar built by Moses in commemoration of the defeat of the Amalekites
senses: Name of altar built by Moses after defeat of Amalekites (Exod 17:15)
Theological declaration: YHWH is the ensign/banner under whom his people fight
Compound: YHWH (H3068G) + nissi my banner (H5251G from nes נֵס)

terms.verses_note: ANCHOR_VERSES_NOT_CAPTURED — follow-up STEP extraction required. Primary anchor: Exod 17:15.

terms.anchor_note: Used as a divine title — YHWH of Hosts/Armies. Conveys God's sovereign command over heavenly and earthly armies. High OT frequency as part of the compound title YHWH Tsva'ot.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: Hosts — as a divine title: the armies under YHWH's command; YHWH of Hosts as sovereign commander of heavenly and earthly forces
senses: The Hosts (of the Lord) — as a divine title/name
Army, host; war; duty/service
LSJ note on sabaōth: transliteration of Hebrew צָבָא 'army' (H6635a). Not in LSJ.
Used in compound YHWH Tsva'ot (Lord of Hosts/Armies)

terms.anchor_note: NT Greek transliteration of Hebrew Tsva'ot. Used in Rom 9:29 and Jas 5:4 — Lord of Sabaoth/Almighty. Carries the full theological weight of YHWH as sovereign over all forces.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: Greek transliteration of Hebrew צָבָא (hosts/armies); used in NT as a title of God — Lord of Sabaoth/Almighty
senses: Transliteration of Hebrew Tsva'ot into Greek
KJV: sabaoth; NIV: Almighty
NT occurrences: Rom 9:29 (citing Isa 1:9); Jas 5:4
LSJ note: transliteration of Hebrew צָבָא 'army'. Not in LSJ as a Greek word.

terms.verses_note: ANCHOR_VERSES_NOT_CAPTURED — follow-up STEP extraction required. Primary anchors: Rom 9:29; Jas 5:4.

terms.anchor_note: As a divine attribute/name: justice, righteousness — what God is in himself. Closely linked to sha.lom in the Psalms (peace and righteousness as paired divine attributes). Foundation of the YHWH-Tsidkenu compound.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: Righteousness, justice — as a divine attribute and name; YHWH himself is righteousness
senses: Righteousness, justice — as a divine name/attribute
§ This name means justice, righteousness
Root of YHWH-Tsidkenu compound (H3072)
Related: peace (sha.lom) — paired with righteousness in Psalms as complementary divine attributes

terms.anchor_note: Used exclusively of God — jealous (only of God). YHWH's jealousy is his intolerance of rival allegiances; it is the covenantal passion that guards the relational ground from which soul-peace flows.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: Jealous — used exclusively as a divine attribute; God's covenantal passion that tolerates no rival
senses: Jealous — used only of God
§ jealous (only of God)
Covenantal term: YHWH's exclusive claim on his people's allegiance
Exod 20:5; 34:14 — primary attestations

terms.verses_note: ANCHOR_VERSES_NOT_CAPTURED — follow-up STEP extraction required. Primary anchors: Exod 20:5; Exod 34:14.

xrefs.xref_note: Aramaic cognate of sha.lom (שָׁלוֹם). Full extraction held in Part 1. Listed here for completeness of the sha.lom semantic cluster at the Part 3 level.
